title: Implement stable persistence storage for media manager

requirements.text: Implement stable storage backend for media assets using Motoko's stable memory. Store uploaded images as blobs in a stable HashMap or StableBuffer to ensure data persists across canister upgrades.

quotes: kita perbaiki media manager dulu agar bisa menyimpan dan upload gambar ke stable persistence storage

acceptanceCriteria: Images uploaded through MediaUploadZone persist across canister upgrades
Backend uses stable storage structures (e.g., stable HashMap) for blob data
Uploaded media can be retrieved by ID after upgrade

requirements.text: Implement the uploadMediaAsset backend method to accept image files (JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP) and PDF documents, validate file types and sizes (max 5MB for images, 10MB for PDFs), store them in stable storage, and return a unique asset ID.

quotes: Backend method uploadMediaAsset not implemented yet

acceptanceCriteria: uploadMediaAsset method accepts blob data, filename, and content type
Method validates file size constraints (5MB images, 10MB PDFs)
Method stores media in stable storage and returns unique asset ID
Method requires valid Internet Identity delegation token

requirements.text: Implement the getAllMediaAssets backend method to retrieve all stored media assets with metadata (ID, filename, content type, upload timestamp, file size).
acceptanceCriteria: getAllMediaAssets returns array of media asset records
Each record includes ID, filename, contentType, timestamp, and size
Method works with both authenticated and public access

requirements.text: Implement the getMediaAssetById backend method to retrieve a specific media asset's blob data and metadata by its unique ID.
acceptanceCriteria: getMediaAssetById accepts asset ID parameter
Method returns blob data and metadata for the specified asset
Method returns null or error for non-existent IDs

requirements.text: Implement the deleteMediaAsset backend method to remove a media asset from stable storage by its ID, requiring authenticated access.
acceptanceCriteria: deleteMediaAsset removes asset from stable storage
Method requires valid Internet Identity delegation token
Method returns success confirmation or error message

requirements.text: Update the MediaUploadZone component to successfully upload images and PDFs through the implemented backend methods, displaying upload progress and success/error feedback.
acceptanceCriteria: File upload triggers backend uploadMediaAsset call
Upload progress is displayed to user
Success toast shown on successful upload
Error toast shown with descriptive message on failure

requirements.text: Update the MediaAssetGrid component to display uploaded media assets from the backend, showing thumbnails for images and appropriate icons for PDFs, with copy ID and delete functionality working correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Grid fetches and displays all media assets from backend
Images display as thumbnails using blob data
PDF files show with document icon
Copy ID button copies asset ID to clipboard
Delete button removes asset from backend and refreshes grid

constraints: Use Motoko stable storage structures (stable HashMap or StableBuffer) for persistence
Respect file size limits: 5MB for images, 10MB for PDFs
Require Internet Identity authentication for upload and delete operations
Support file types: JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP, PDF only
nonGoals: External storage integration (AWS S3, Cloudinary, etc.)
Advanced permissions or access control beyond authentication
